instance: legacy Step-5 instance (v4 preview verifier, 3-seat panel; initial-draw and quorum-miss recovery are not deployed)
note: Historical Step-5 deployment. Its WorldIDGate calls a World ID 4.0 verifier that official documentation currently classifies as preview; it is evidence of an on-chain experiment, not a supported production dependency. This immutable bytecode also predates party-aware admission, bounded initial-draw unwind, bounded quorum recovery, Permit2 re-bonding, immutable voting durations, and the replacement source's 20 MUSD question fee. Its fixed question fee remains 2 MUSD and its deployer-controlled duration setter remains present. Do not run the capstone against these addresses. Deploy and source-verify a fresh instance with WorldIDRouterGate, update the app/keeper/World Developer Portal permissions, and record it separately.

recommendedReplacementParams.reason: one spare eligible juror tolerates one pre-draw withdrawal; immutable five-minute windows and fee match the replacement source

recommendedReplacementSybilGate.reason: uses the officially documented World Chain mainnet Router and removes the preview v4 verifier from the production dependency set

step35DeriskInstance.note: Historical Step-3.5 human-free de-risk against the v4 STAGING preview verifier (Simulator proofs, no Orb). These traces prove the adapter behavior but are not production-readiness evidence. Three enforcement traces are in docs/DEMO.md.
